authors: Mingchen Zhuge1,2, Changsheng Zhao1, Dylan R. Ashley2, Wenyi Wang2, Dmitrii Khizbullin2, Yunyang Xiong1, Zechun Liu1, Ernie Chang1, Raghuraman Krishnamoorthi1, Yuandong Tian1, Yangyang Shi1, Vikas Chandra1, Jürgen Schmidhuber2

content: Advances in multimodal agentic systems address real-world challenges.
Current evaluation methods are inadequate, focusing on final outcomes or requiring extensive manual labor.
Lack of feedback during intermediate task-solving stages in non-traditional systems.
Need for a more comprehensive and efficient evaluation framework.
Introduction of Agent-as-a-Judge to provide rich and cost-effective evaluation.
Current benchmarks do not reflect realistic challenges in code generation.
Evaluation methods focus only on final outcomes, missing intermediate feedback.
Manual evaluation is time-consuming and expensive.
Need for a benchmark that covers a wide range of real-world tasks.
Agent-as-a-Judge framework extends LLM-as-a-Judge.
Provides rich intermediate feedback throughout task-solving.
Evaluates code generation tasks effectively.
Development of DevAI benchmark with 55 real-world AI development tasks.
Evaluation of MetaGPT, GPT-Pilot, and OpenHands on DevAI.
Comparison of Agent-as-a-Judge with LLM-as-a-Judge and human evaluators.
Agent-as-a-Judge aligns 90% with human evaluators, outperforming LLM-as-a-Judge (70%).
Saves 97.72% of time and 97.64% of cost compared to human evaluation.
DevAI presents a high level of challenge, with only one full task satisfied by evaluated systems.
Agent-as-a-Judge provides valuable intermediate feedback.
Cost-effective and time-efficient compared to human evaluation.
High alignment with human evaluators in complex tasks.
Agent-as-a-Judge marks a significant step forward in evaluating agentic systems.
Framework is cost-effective and aligns closely with human evaluation.
Viable replacement for manual evaluators in real-world applications.

Fig1.caption: Figure 1 We introduce the Agent-as-a-Judge framework wherein agentic systems are used to evaluate agentic systems.

Fig6.caption: Figure 1 We introduce the Agent-as-a-Judge framework wherein agentic systems are used to evaluate agentic systems.
